summary: Iteration-13 retest of P2 waiting-room enum patch BLOCKED. The patch SQL (/app/supabase/p2_waiting_room_fix_enum.sql) was only PARTIALLY applied to the live Supabase DB. The CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION portion clearly ran (the function now uses 'waiting'::attempt_status literals, evidenced by the new error text), but the prerequisite `ALTER TYPE attempt_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'waiting';` did NOT commit. Result: the redeem_test_code RPC still returns HTTP 400 from the live PostgREST at https://soaagmzmecutvlxfbscl.supabase.co/rest/v1/rpc/redeem_test_code with body `invalid input value for enum attempt_status: "waiting"`. Steps 1-2 PASS (proctor code grab=YQJ9C6, student reaches /student/test-code, code-aggregated fill works, code-submit fires the RPC). Step 3 FAILS: no /student/waiting redirect, code-error panel correctly surfaces 'invalid input value for enum attempt_status: "waiting"'. Steps 4-9 are BLOCKED. Step 10 smoke: /admin/tests not exercised because the test stopped at the blocker.

critical.issue: HTTP 400 -- response body: `invalid input value for enum attempt_status: "waiting"`. The ALTER TYPE statement at line 15 of /app/supabase/p2_waiting_room_fix_enum.sql did NOT actually commit to the live DB, but the CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION at lines 18+ DID (proof: the error text changed from iteration_12's `column "status" is of type attempt_status but expression is of type text` to the new enum-value error, which can only happen if the function is now casting `::attempt_status` -- which it does on lines 102-104 and 163). The enum on the live DB is still `('in_progress','submitted','reset')`.

action_items: Re-run ONLY the first statement of /app/supabase/p2_waiting_room_fix_enum.sql on the live Supabase SQL editor: `ALTER TYPE attempt_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'waiting';`. Run it on its OWN, NOT inside a transaction block. Note: in Postgres < 12 (and even with some Supabase tooling) `ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE` cannot be used in the same transaction as a query that consumes the new value -- the typical Supabase SQL Editor wraps multi-statement scripts in a transaction, which silently rolls back the ALTER TYPE if any later statement errors, OR commits it only at the end (so the same-batch function recreation could see a stale catalog). Safest fix: run the ALTER TYPE ALONE first, click Run, wait for success, THEN run the CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION as a second batch.

After the ALTER TYPE truly commits, verify with: `SELECT unnest(enum_range(NULL::attempt_status));` -- expect 4 rows including 'waiting'. Then re-run iteration_13 playwright sequence (same student 300432, same section 8bdcad7b, same test c3bdbef0, daily code re-grab from proctor).
Consider also: add `commit;` between the ALTER TYPE and the CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION in p2_waiting_room_fix_enum.sql to force the enum addition to land before the function is recompiled.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/supabase/p2_waiting_room_fix_enum.sql -- the two statements (ALTER TYPE then CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION) should be separated by `commit;` and a fresh `begin;` (or run separately) so the enum addition is durable before the function references the new value. Many SQL editors run scripts inside an implicit transaction and Postgres requires ADD VALUE to be committed before being used in the same transaction (pg < 12 strict; pg >= 12 more lenient but still inconsistent across drivers).
Frontend handling at [data-testid=code-error] continues to correctly render the raw Postgres error verbatim -- good for debugging but consider a friendlier copy in production once stable.

success_rate.backend: RPC still 400 (same root-cause family as iteration_12, partial patch)
success_rate.frontend: Iteration-12 frontend fixes still verified 100% (code-aggregated fill, code-submit click, error surfacing). Steps 1-2 of the requested flow PASS. Steps 3-10 BLOCKED by Supabase enum patch not applied.

rca_of_the_issue: RCA -- iteration_13: The patch file /app/supabase/p2_waiting_room_fix_enum.sql contains two statements, (1) `ALTER TYPE attempt_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'waiting';` and (2) `CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.redeem_test_code(...)` whose body contains `'waiting'::attempt_status` literal casts. The user reported running the patch. Observed behavior at run time: redeem_test_code now returns 400 with body `invalid input value for enum attempt_status: "waiting"` -- a DIFFERENT error than iteration_12's `column "status" is of type attempt_status but expression is of type text`. The difference proves: the FUNCTION body was updated (it is now performing the explicit ::attempt_status cast, otherwise we'd still see the text-vs-enum error from iteration_12), BUT the enum itself still does NOT contain the value 'waiting'. This is consistent with two scenarios: (a) the SQL editor wrapped both statements in a transaction and the ALTER TYPE silently rolled back / was not visible to subsequent statements (pg pre-12 behavior or some Supabase tooling quirks), or (b) only the CREATE OR REPLACE was actually executed. Mitigation: run the ALTER TYPE statement ALONE (single statement, separate execution), verify with `SELECT enum_range(NULL::attempt_status);` returning four labels including 'waiting', THEN the existing function works as-is. Reproduction: super@hpa.test -> /admin/sections/8bdcad7b... -> proctor-launch-c3bdbef0-...-EOC -> grab YQJ9C6 from [data-testid=proctor-code]. Student 300432 -> /student/login -> Pre-AP Biology 1 tile -> teacher-verify-yes -> test-pick-c3bdbef0-...-EOC -> /student/test-code/.../EOC -> page.fill('[data-testid=code-aggregated]','YQJ9C6') -> page.click('[data-testid=code-submit]'). Network tab: POST /rest/v1/rpc/redeem_test_code returns 400 with body `{"code":"22P02","message":"invalid input value for enum attempt_status: \"waiting\""}` (or similar). UI: [data-testid=code-error] displays `invalid input value for enum attempt_status: "waiting"`.
